topic: Application and CLI Hot-Update and Version Control System
context: We are designing the client auto-update engine and local CLI tool version detection system for OMNIX DevFlow.
options_considered: Require users to manually re-download the desktop application and CLI binaries from GitHub.

Implement an integrated Auto-Updater Service that checks a central remote manifest, fetches delta/patch releases, signature-verifies download payloads, and performs silent background hot-swaps for both the main client and internal CLI scripts (skills).
chosen_option: Implement an integrated Auto-Updater Service with signature verification for the application client, and a dynamic semver comparison system for internal CLI scripts.
reasoning: For the App Client: If we choose Tauri, we will leverage the native signature-verified Tauri auto-updater. If Electron, we will use electron-updater. Both pull from a central manifest file (e.g., update.json).

For CLIs / Skills: Because skills are plain folders with metadata files (e.g. SKILL.md containing a version string), we can easily compare local semver against the remote repository version via HTTP and trigger background node/python package manager installations (like bun or uv) or pull git delta patches to execute silent hot-updates.
tradeoffs: Silent background updates require user consent or configurations to avoid disrupting developer workflows during critical coding tasks.
impact_scope: Skill/CLI manager (version checks and git-pull updater)
future_risks: Network restrictions blocking update server endpoints: mitigated by supporting multiple mirror registries (e.g., GitHub releases and CNB/Gitee mirrors).
